title: QUE HAY PUEBLA
subtitle: Full-stack B2B SaaS directory platform with tiered subscriptions, interactive zone mapping, and a fully automated billing lifecycle.
about:
What is QUE HAY PUEBLA?

Puebla's small businesses — restaurants, cafés, shops, and services — have no dedicated digital infrastructure. QueHay Puebla fills that gap with a geo-segmented discovery platform where business owners self-onboard, choose a subscription tier, and gain immediate access to an analytics dashboard tracking every customer interaction. The platform is built to scale across Mexico's mid-sized cities with zero operational overhead per new business added.

challenge.title: The Challenge: No Digital Infrastructure for Local Commerce
challenge.description: Local businesses in mid-sized Mexican cities lack the tools, budget, and technical skill to maintain an online presence. A platform serving them had to be friction-free for non-technical owners, financially sustainable through subscriptions, and resilient enough to handle geographic expansion without engineering intervention.

cards.title: Discovery Gap
cards.description: 120,000+ businesses in Puebla's metro area had no dedicated local search layer — consumers relied on fragmented WhatsApp groups and word-of-mouth.

cards.title: Manual Onboarding Bottleneck
cards.description: Any directory requiring manual admin entry per business cannot scale. The platform needed self-service onboarding with automated approval and billing activation.

cards.title: Monetization Without Friction
cards.description: Charging small businesses requires trust. The system needed transparent tier-based pricing, free trials, and a self-managed billing portal — all without a sales call.

solution.title: The Solution: A Self-Operating Geographic SaaS
solution.description: The platform handles the full lifecycle autonomously — from a business owner's first registration to their Stripe subscription renewal — while exposing analytics, media management, and tier-gated features through a clean role-based dashboard.

features.title: 4-Tier Subscription Engine
features.description: Stripe-powered tiers (Starter free, PRO $299, ELITE $599, PATROCINADOR $999 MXN/mo) with 7-day trials, annual billing discounts, and a self-service Customer Portal for upgrades and cancellations.

features.title: Zone-Based Interactive Map
features.description: Leaflet map with real-time clustering across 8 city zones. Business pins are visually differentiated by tier, and a Haversine-based distance filter sorts results by proximity.

features.title: Tier-Gated Feature System
features.description: A centralized tierLimits config gates every feature — photo limits, analytics frequency, delivery links, Instagram mentions, and the 'Sorpréndeme' random-discovery engine — by subscription level.

features.title: Real-Time Analytics Dashboard
features.description: Tracks VIEW, CLICK_PHONE, CLICK_WHATSAPP, CLICK_WEBSITE, CLICK_DIRECTIONS, and SHARE events per business with frequency scaled to subscription tier (daily for Patrocinador, weekly for Elite, monthly for Pro).

techStack.title: Technical Depth: Architecture & Infrastructure
techStack.description: Built as a production-grade Next.js 15 App Router application with server actions, edge middleware, and a multi-provider auth layer on top of a relational database.

categories.name: Full-Stack Framework
categories.description: Next.js 15 App Router with React Server Components and Server Actions. Middleware protects all /panel routes with JWT session validation via NextAuth.js v5.

categories.name: Auth & RBAC
categories.description: NextAuth.js v5 with three providers: Google OAuth, Facebook OAuth, and Email/Password (bcryptjs). Three roles: CONSUMER, BUSINESS_OWNER, ADMIN — enforced at the middleware and API layer.

categories.name: Billing Infrastructure
categories.description: Stripe Checkout Sessions, webhook listeners for subscription lifecycle events, and the Customer Billing Portal. Trial periods and tier changes are handled entirely via webhook processing.

categories.name: Security & Rate Limiting
categories.description: Upstash Redis-backed rate limiting with three profiles: strict (5 req/min for auth), standard (20 req/min for mutations), and relaxed (100 req/min for reads). All inputs validated with Zod.

results.title: Zero admin overhead
results.description: per new business. Onboarding, approval, subscription activation, and analytics are fully automated end-to-end.

results.title: Full billing lifecycle automated
results.description: Stripe webhooks handle trial starts, upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations with instant tier enforcement across the platform.

results.title: Production SEO infrastructure
results.description: Dynamic sitemap, Schema.org LocalBusiness JSON-LD, Open Graph tags, and an llms.txt file for AI agent discoverability — all generated server-side per business slug.
